name: NormEDSNoConsecutiveZero
why_it_matters: The N5 scalar obligation (notes/DIVISION_POLY_TORSION_MAP.md): no two consecutive zeros in the curve-specialized scalar EDS w = normEDS beta Psi3(x0) prePsi4(x0) over the algebraic closure. Via the landed descent (secp256k1_exists_normEDS_consecutive_eq_zero_of_not_isCoprime) it closes IsCoprime(Phi n, PsiSq n) for secp256k1 — the coprimality input on the critical path N5 -> N7 -> N10 -> N11 -> N13 toward #E[n] = n^2 and E[n] = (Z/n)^2, which feeds Weil-pairing non-degeneracy (W5). Unblocked by the in-repo L4 port: normEDS_isEllSequence + normEDS_somos4 supply the index-propagation engine; the pairwise coprimality certificates (L5/L6/L6b) close the degenerate low-index cases.
hint: Anchor: normEDS_one (w 1 = 1). Degenerate triangle: beta = 0 iff Psi2Sq(x0) = 0 (field, hβ); then CoprimePsi2Psi3 (L5) gives Psi3(x0) ≠ 0 and CoprimePsi2PrePsi4 (L6b) gives prePsi4(x0) ≠ 0; CoprimePsi3PrePsi4 (L6) excludes c = d = 0. Propagation: from w n = w (n+1) = 0 use normEDS_somos4 and instances of normEDS_isEllSequence (IsEllSequence three-term identity) to force zeros at lower indices, descending to a contradiction with w 1 = 1 (strong induction on n). Expect case splits on parity and on which of b, c, d vanishes. This is a MID-size single-file effort (~150-400 lines), not a tier-0 target.
verified_note: Discharged as theorem `secp256k1_normEDS_no_consecutive_zero` in Ecdlp/Proved/DivisionPolynomialCoprime.lean — the scalar half stated in its own right (with `1 ≤ n` kept only for signature compatibility; the proof covers every n : ℤ). It is a direct instance of the abstract Ward apparition-rigidity theorem `Ecdlp.NormEDS.normEDS_not_consecutive_zeros` (Ecdlp/Proved/NormEDSConsecutiveZeros.lean), its two nondegeneracy side conditions supplied by the L5/L6 coprimality certificates at the closure. The same discharge, applied through the landed descent, closes the actual node in the flagship theorem `secp256k1_isCoprime_Φ_ΨSq` of that module (IsCoprime(Φ n, ΨSq n) for secp256k1, every n : ℤ). Curve-agnostic Ward rigidity ported from the reviewed candidate that appeared in draft PR #172, then reassembled into small reviewable modules per notes/reviews/PR172_SPLIT_PLAN.md (Split-PR 1).
